name: convex
description: Umbrella skill for all Convex development patterns. Routes to specific skills like convex-functions, convex-realtime, convex-agents, etc.
capabilities: Routes to specialized Convex skills for functions, schema, realtime, and agents
Provides quick access to security audit and best practices patterns
Links to official Convex documentation for comprehensive guidance
Organizes Convex development into logical skill categories
author: Convex
securitySummary: All 18 static findings are false positives. SKILL.md is a documentation file containing skill command references and documentation URLs. No code execution, shell commands, or actual network calls exist in this file. Static scanner misidentified forward slashes (/) in skill commands like /convex-functions as shell backticks.
valueStatement: Convex is a serverless backend platform that combines a database, serverless functions, and reactive subscriptions. This skill routes you to specialized patterns for functions, schema validation, realtime updates, AI agents, and security audits.
limitations: This is a routing skill that delegates to other specialized skills
Does not execute Convex functions or interact with a live database
Does not generate complete Convex projects from scratch

useCases.title: Building Serverless Backend Functions
useCases.description: Write queries, mutations, and actions for your application backend using Convex functions pattern.

useCases.title: Designing Database Schemas
useCases.description: Define and validate database schemas with proper data types, indexes, and constraints.

useCases.title: Adding Realtime Updates
useCases.description: Implement reactive subscriptions that automatically update when database data changes.

promptTemplates.title: Getting Started with Convex
promptTemplates.prompt: I want to build a Convex backend for my application. Start with the best practices skill to help me understand the core patterns.
promptTemplates.scenario: Initial setup and learning Convex fundamentals

promptTemplates.title: Writing Backend Logic
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me write a Convex function that handles user authentication and stores user data in the database.
promptTemplates.scenario: Creating queries and mutations for user management

promptTemplates.title: Implementing Realtime Features
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a realtime subscription that updates the UI whenever new messages are added to a chat room.

promptTemplates.title: Security Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Review my Convex application for security issues. Run a security audit on my function implementations.
promptTemplates.scenario: Checking for vulnerabilities in production code

bestPractices: Start with /convex-best-practices to understand core patterns before building
Use /convex-schema-validator to define proper data validation upfront
Use /convex-functions for all backend logic implementation
antiPatterns: Avoid writing complex business logic directly in frontend code
Do not skip schema validation for user inputs
Avoid making synchronous API calls inside Convex queries

faq.question: What is Convex?
faq.answer: Convex is a serverless backend platform that provides a database, serverless functions, and realtime subscriptions in one unified system.

faq.question: How do I create my first Convex function?
faq.answer: Use the /convex-functions skill for patterns on writing queries, mutations, and actions for your backend logic.

faq.question: Can Convex handle realtime updates?
faq.answer: Yes. Use /convex-realtime to learn how to build subscriptions that push updates to clients automatically when data changes.

faq.question: How do I validate incoming data?
faq.answer: Use /convex-schema-validator to define validators with the Valibot library for type-safe data validation.

faq.question: Is Convex secure?
faq.answer: Convex includes built-in authentication helpers. Use /convex-security-audit to review your application for security issues.

faq.question: Can I use Convex for AI agent development?
faq.answer: Yes. Use /convex-agents to learn patterns for building AI agents with tools and function calling capabilities.
